title: Neurodynamical Computing at the Information Boundaries of Intelligent Systems.

assets.abstract: Artificial intelligence has not achieved defining features of biological intelligence despite models boasting more parameters than neurons in the human brain. In this perspective article, we synthesize historical approaches to understanding intelligent systems and argue that methodological and epistemic biases in these fields can be resolved by shifting away from cognitivist brain-as-computer theories and recognizing that brains exist within large, interdependent living systems. Integrating the

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind.
